issues.body: termKey concatenates attacker/result-controlled lexical value, datatype, and language fields with raw NUL delimiters. Those fields are not escaped or length-prefixed, so distinct literals such as value `a\u0000b` with datatype `c` and value `a` with datatype `b\u0000c` produce the same key. deriveGraph then merges their nodes and may aggregate edges, violating the stated guarantee that every displayed node preserves a distinct real term. The raw NULs also make Git classify this TypeScript source as binary, hiding the implementation from normal textual diff review.
issues.fix_hint: Use an injective structural encoding, for example JSON.stringify of a fixed tuple `[type, value, datatype ?? "", xmlLang ?? ""]` or a length-prefixed encoding, and add a unit test with delimiter-containing lexical/datatype fields proving that the terms remain distinct. Avoid embedding raw NUL bytes in the source file.
issues.title: NUL-delimited term keys are collision-prone

issues.body: ResultGraphView and deriveGraph are static imports, so all graph-rendering and derivation code is bundled whenever the lazy hero runner loads, even for users who never select Graph. This conflicts with the repository's explicit policy that net-new uncertain or rarely used frontend features increasing bundle size load through a literal dynamic import only when invoked. The source comment acknowledges the bundle impact but does not implement invocation-path loading.
issues.fix_hint: Move the optional renderer behind next/dynamic or React.lazy and load its graph-specific implementation with a literal import() when the user invokes the Graph view. Keep only the minimum cheap eligibility logic in the core hero chunk, and update the relevant bundle check if this creates a material optional chunk.
issues.title: Optional graph feature is eagerly included in the hero chunk

verdict.summary: The graph derivation can merge distinct RDF terms because its identity encoding is not injective, which can invent node/edge coalescing contrary to the feature's load-bearing honesty claim. The optional graph renderer is also statically imported into the hero chunk instead of being loaded on invocation as required by the repository's frontend optional-code policy. The supplied tests do not cover the identity collision.
